svg.title: Dual-Key Compromise — DoS Not Theft
svg.desc: Animated diagram showing how OP_VAULT's worst case — trigger key plus recoveryauth key compromised — results in a denial-of-service loop but NOT fund theft, because recovery always goes to the pre-committed immutable address.

g.aria-label: Watchtower recovery attempt — slower, gets front-run by attacker
Attacker front-runs with stolen recoveryauth key
Result: denial of service only, not fund theft — recovery address is immutable
Attack loop: funds cycle back to vault, attacker can repeat indefinitely
g.text: attacker can repeat — funds cycle back

g.aria-label: Comparison: CTV and CAT+CSFS dual-key compromise leads to fund theft, not just DoS
g.text: In CTV & CAT+CSFS: hot + cold key compromised = FUND THEFT (cold key bypasses all constraints)
OP_VAULT uniquely survives dual-key compromise — immutable recovery address prevents theft

g.aria-label: Watchtower recovery blocked — attacker's recoveryauth is faster
